note: worktree was dirty at task creation

Decision: changes_requested (revision 1). The implementation is well-scoped and the claimed suites pass, but six contract blockers remain. 1) canonicalJson is publicly exported without runtime validation. Direct probe canonicalJson({x: NaN}) returned {"x":null}, violating the fail-closed Canonical JSON contract. Make the public entrypoint validate unknown input, keep any unchecked serializer private, and add direct canonicalJson negative tests rather than only testing checksumCanonicalJson. 2) finite inputs can produce non-finite durable outputs. Direct probe with baseline=-Number.MAX_VALUE and comparison=Number.MAX_VALUE returned rawDelta=Infinity. Validate normalization spans, aggregate weight sums, normalized values, raw/normalized deltas, coverage, contributions and score before returning; fail closed and add overflow regressions. Also align config validation with canonical checksum rules so unsafe integer config values are rejected by the config validator rather than later during checksum serialization. 3) formal rule summary accepts similarityScore=null and emits a summary for a coverage-insufficient result. V005 comparison_run.similarity_score is NOT NULL and S047 says blocked/failed computations do not create a Run, so a persisted formal Run rule summary must require a finite 0-100 score. Add a null rejection test. 4) rule-summary.ts uses an unapproved hardcoded 90 threshold to split similarities and differences. S092 permits rule thresholds only from released config, while the production policy is not_released. Remove threshold-based classification until a released versioned policy supplies it; the current test only proves opportunities is empty and misses this second hardcoded threshold. 5) algorithm EvidenceQualityStatus adds insufficient, but V005 comparison_dimension_evidence.quality_status only permits ready|limited. Do not create a persisted-looking qualityStatus contract that W05 cannot write. Align with V005 and represent algorithm/policy eligibility separately if quality_insufficient must be selected; add a contract test proving the projection maps cleanly to approved persistence semantics. 6) evidence-manifest ordering uses localeCompare over arbitrary record IDs, which is locale/ICU dependent and therefore unsuitable for a durable canonical checksum. Use an explicit UTF-16 code-unit comparator and add non-ASCII ordering/checksum stability coverage. Independent validation: typecheck passed; algorithm contract 11/11 passed when rerun outside the sandbox because tsx IPC creation was sandbox-blocked; schema contract 30/30 passed; guard:worktree, git diff --check, and protected-path status passed. The passing 11 tests omit the demonstrated negative boundaries. Memory Used: 'Algorithm checksum must include every behavior-affecting config field' affected the checksum/config validation audit and the requirement to test every behavior-affecting boundary; 'Update controller notes whenever checksum/contract shape changes' affected comparison of docs/notes-model.md with the actual exported contract. Memory Candidates: none.

Decision: changes_requested (revision 2). Six prior blockers are closed and all claimed validation passes, but two contract blockers remain. 1) Canonical JSON accepts sparse arrays: direct probe canonicalJson(new Array(1)) returned [] and therefore collides with a true empty array. Reject array holes explicitly at validation or define a non-colliding approved representation, and add direct canonical/checksum regressions for leading, middle, and trailing holes. 2) The exported algorithm still accepts normalization.clamp=false and can emit finite but V005-incompatible durable values. Direct probe with min=0, max=1, baseline=0, comparison=2 returned comparisonNormalizedValue=200 and normalizedDelta=200, while V005 permits normalized values only 0..100 and delta only -100..100. Make every successful algorithm result projectable to V005, preferably by requiring clamp=true unless an approved contract change says otherwise, and add projection boundary tests. Independent validation passed: typecheck, algorithm contract 14/14, schema contract 30/30, guard:worktree, git diff --check, and protected paths. Original direct probes now reject canonical NaN, rawDelta overflow, and null formal similarityScore. Memory Used: Algorithm checksum must include every behavior-affecting config field affected the audit of clamp semantics as checksum-covered behavior; Update controller notes whenever checksum/contract shape changes requires docs/notes-model.md to stay aligned if clamp semantics change. Memory lifecycle use_count incremented to 2 for both entries; last_used_at remains 2026-07-19 and expires_at remains 2026-10-17.

Decision: approved. Revision 2 closes both remaining blockers: Canonical JSON rejects leading, middle, and trailing sparse array holes before serialization/checksum, and algorithm config requires normalization.clamp=true so successful normalized values and deltas project into V005 bounds. Independent validation passed: typecheck, algorithm contract 15/15, schema contract 30/30, guard:worktree, git diff --check, and protected paths. Direct probes reject canonical NaN, sparse arrays, rawDelta overflow, clamp=false, and null formal similarityScore. No DB, HTTP, adapter, production thresholds, or WorkPLS identity entered T0038 scope. Out-of-scope diffs were accepted only as verified pre-existing T0036/T0037/controller artifacts and unrelated .mimocode local state. Memory Used: Algorithm checksum must include every behavior-affecting config field affected verification that clamp semantics remain checksum-covered; Update controller notes whenever checksum/contract shape changes affected verification that docs/notes-model.md records sparse-array and clamp contract changes. Both memory entries use_count advanced to 3; last_used_at 2026-07-19; expires_at 2026-10-17.
